name: Band-Resisted Neck Extension
resistance_profile: Band tension rises as the head extends back, so it is hardest near full extension (peak contraction) and lightest at the stretch.
cues: Nod the head back through a smooth arc
Keep the shoulders down and the torso still
Stop at neutral rather than cranking the neck back
execution_steps: Anchor a light band in front of you at about head height, or loop it and hold the free end.
Place the band across your forehead and face the anchor in a tall, braced stance.
Let the band draw your chin down toward your chest to a gentle stretch.
Drive your head back, extending the neck to a neutral or slightly-back position against the band.
Pause, then lower the chin back to the start slowly.
good_when: You want to train the posterior neck extensors without a head harness
You are building neck size and strength for contact sports or postural work
bad_when: You want maximal loading, since band resistance is capped and lightest at the stretch
You have acute neck pain or known cervical pathology
common_errors: Leaning the whole torso back instead of extending the neck
progressions: Step back to increase band tension
Add reps or move to a heavier band
